summary_one_line: Input must parse as namespaced XML so the document carries a non-NULL oldNs list reached at free time.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed XML prolog (<?xml version="1.0"?>, DOCTYPE) followed by namespace-bearing constructs (xmlns / xlink:href) so the parser builds a document whose cur->oldNs list is non-NULL when xmlTextReaderFreeDoc frees it.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds carry intact structural literals at fixed offsets: the '<?xml version="1' header at 0x10-0x1f and 'DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1' at 0x20+, plus xmlns(xlink and xlink:href tokens; these drive a successful parse that constructs the namespace list, and on the branch the winner takes the True side (T=4 F=0).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds corrupt the leading/structural bytes (e.g. 0x00 run, '88888I"1', long '////' runs at offset 0) so the prolog/namespace machinery never builds an oldNs list; on the blocker line the loser is T=0 F=10, never taking the True side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads logged CMP operands (the XML keyword/literal byte sequences the parser compares against) from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the expected operand at matching input offsets, repairing the structural literals so the parse reaches namespace construction; naive lacks CMP introspection and must hit the same multi-byte literals by blind havoc.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the True side of a NULL-check on cur->oldNs inside the document-free path.
On the blocker line the winner always takes the True side while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 496  T=4 F=0  T=0 F=10  if (cur->oldNs != NULL) xmlFreeNsList(cur->oldNs);

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds contain an intact XML prolog and DOCTYPE structural literals.
Winner seeds carry xmlns / xlink namespace tokens that drive namespace-list construction.
Loser seeds corrupt the leading bytes so the structural parse never establishes the namespace list.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which scans the input for one logged CMP operand and substitutes the other at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S and must reach the structural literals by blind havoc/token mutation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 7250 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 51fd9b36079604e3` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 3 and depth 4 of the ancestor chain, the floor signal for an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating the same oldNs-non-NULL branch behind a multi-byte XML literal where cmplog and naive resolve at statistically indistinguishable rates would refute the I2S attribution.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte diff shows W/L differing across nearly all offsets (frame shift from BytesDelete/Insert), so I cannot isolate one CMP operand offset that I2S specifically repaired.
